L1.area: Plugin lifecycle management and system time synchronization logic

L2.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled datetime values received via WebSocket messages are directly interpolated into shell commands without sanitization, allowing authenticated users (or unauthenticated users if security is disabled) to execute arbitrary commands on the server.

L3.area: System time setting plugin and its datetime processing and OS clock synchronization logic
L3.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled datetime values received via WebSocket delta messages are directly interpolated into shell commands used to set the system clock, without validation or sanitization. When the plugin is enabled and processes incoming navigation datetime data, an attacker can inject arbitrary shell commands that execute with the privileges of the server process or potentially root via sudo.

ground_truth.reason: The datetime value from navigation.datetime is directly interpolated into a shell command string executed via spawn('sh', ['-c', ...]) without any validation or sanitization, allowing arbitrary command injection.
